nodes.label: squared-goodness deactivation cascade
nodes.rationale: Sum-squared goodness has an h-factor in its gradient that deactivates/freezes quiet units, causing a dead-unit cascade compounding with depth. Linear goodness removes the h-factor, keeping units alive.

nodes.label: P2.1 — the normalization x goodness grid (make-or-break gate)
nodes.rationale: 9-cell grid crossing {length/layer/group/online-BN/batch/no-norm} x {squared,linear} goodness under threshold-free contrast loss on CIFAR-10-flat, 5 seeds. Pass gate: any learning cell reaching depth-slope >= 0.

nodes.label: P2.1's 9-cell grid
nodes.rationale: Cells: len+sq(wall)=-0.010, len+lin=-0.019, layer+lin(HERO)=-0.020, layer+sq=COLLAPSED, group+lin=-0.017, online-BN+lin(HERO)=-0.021, batch-BN+lin=-0.026, batch-BN+sq(Trifecta)=COLLAPSED, none+lin=near-chance fail.

nodes.label: P2.1 verdict: GATE FAILED — 0/7 learning cells reach slope >= 0
nodes.rationale: Transmission fix (linear goodness + per-sample norm) works mechanically (dead-units->0, erank restored) but produces NO depth-rising class separability.

nodes.label: squared goodness + mean-zero norm = catastrophic unit death
nodes.rationale: layer-norm/batch-norm combined with squared goodness kills nearly all units (dead 1.00, erank 0); length-norm survives the wall only because it does not remove the mean.
